title: Clean Energy Innovation Act
bill_text: A BILL To provide for the establishment of a clean energy research program, and for other purposes. Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representatives of the United States of America in Congress assembled, SECTION 1. SHORT TITLE. This Act may be cited as the 'Clean Energy Innovation Act'. SEC. 2. CLEAN ENERGY RESEARCH PROGRAM. (a) ESTABLISHMENT.—The Secretary of Energy shall establish a program to support research and development of clean energy technologies. (b) FOCUS AREAS.—The program shall focus on— (1) advanced solar photovoltaic technologies; (2) next-generation battery storage; (3) green hydrogen production; (4) carbon capture and sequestration.
summary: This bill creates a federal research program for clean energy technologies. The Department of Energy would fund research in four areas: better solar panels, improved batteries for storing energy, producing hydrogen fuel cleanly, and capturing carbon emissions. The goal is to develop new technologies to reduce greenhouse gas emissions.

title: Rural Healthcare Access Act
bill_text: A BILL To improve access to healthcare services in rural areas. Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representatives of the United States of America in Congress assembled, SECTION 1. SHORT TITLE. This Act may be cited as the 'Rural Healthcare Access Act'. SEC. 2. FINDINGS. Congress finds that— (1) rural communities face significant barriers to healthcare access; (2) many rural hospitals have closed in recent years. SEC. 3. GRANTS FOR RURAL HEALTH CLINICS. (a) IN GENERAL.—The Secretary of Health and Human Services shall award grants to eligible entities to establish or expand rural health clinics. (b) AUTHORIZATION.—There is authorized to be appropriated $500,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2024 through 2029.
summary: This bill provides $500 million per year for six years to help build and expand health clinics in rural areas. It responds to the problem of rural hospital closures by funding smaller clinics that can provide basic healthcare services to underserved communities.

title: Student Loan Relief Act
bill_text: A BILL To provide relief from student loan debt. Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representatives of the United States of America in Congress assembled, SECTION 1. SHORT TITLE. This Act may be cited as the 'Student Loan Relief Act'. SEC. 2. INCOME-DRIVEN REPAYMENT ENHANCEMENT. (a) PAYMENT CAP.—No borrower shall be required to pay more than 5 percent of discretionary income toward federal student loans. (b) FORGIVENESS.—The Secretary of Education shall forgive the remaining balance of a federal student loan after the borrower has made 240 monthly payments. SEC. 3. INTEREST RATE REDUCTION. All federal student loans shall accrue interest at a rate not to exceed 3 percent.
summary: This bill makes three changes to federal student loans: (1) caps monthly payments at 5% of discretionary income, (2) forgives remaining balances after 20 years of payments, and (3) limits interest rates to 3%. These changes would reduce the burden on borrowers with federal student loan debt.

title: Cybersecurity Workforce Development Act
bill_text: A BILL To establish a program to develop the cybersecurity workforce. SECTION 1. SHORT TITLE. This Act may be cited as the 'Cybersecurity Workforce Development Act'. SEC. 2. SCHOLARSHIP PROGRAM. (a) ESTABLISHMENT.—The Director of the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency shall establish a scholarship program for students pursuing degrees in cybersecurity. (b) SERVICE REQUIREMENT.—A scholarship recipient shall work for a Federal agency in a cybersecurity position for a period equal to the duration of the scholarship. SEC. 3. APPRENTICESHIP PROGRAM. The Secretary of Labor shall establish an apprenticeship program for cybersecurity positions in critical infrastructure sectors.
summary: This bill creates two programs to train more cybersecurity workers: (1) a scholarship program where students get free education in exchange for working at a federal agency afterward, and (2) an apprenticeship program for hands-on cybersecurity training in critical infrastructure like power grids and water systems.

title: Prescription Drug Pricing Transparency Act
bill_text: A BILL To increase transparency in prescription drug pricing. SECTION 1. SHORT TITLE. This Act may be cited as the 'Prescription Drug Pricing Transparency Act'. SEC. 2. PRICE INCREASE REPORTING. (a) REQUIREMENT.—A manufacturer that increases the wholesale acquisition cost of a prescription drug by more than 10 percent in any 12-month period shall submit to the Secretary a report justifying the increase. (b) PUBLIC DISCLOSURE.—The Secretary shall make such reports available to the public. SEC. 3. PHARMACY BENEFIT MANAGER TRANSPARENCY. Pharmacy benefit managers shall disclose to health plans the rebates received from drug manufacturers.
summary: This bill requires drug companies to publicly explain any price increases over 10% in a year. It also requires pharmacy benefit managers (the middlemen between drug makers and insurance companies) to disclose the rebates they receive. The goal is to make drug pricing more transparent so consumers and policymakers understand why prices are rising.

title: Workforce Training and Apprenticeship Act
bill_text: A BILL To expand apprenticeships, short-term training grants, and employer partnerships for in-demand jobs.
summary: This bill creates federal grants to expand apprenticeships and short-term job training programs. It also encourages employers to partner with schools and workforce boards so workers can move into in-demand jobs more quickly.

title: Veteran Home Loan Refinancing Act
bill_text: A BILL To allow eligible veterans to refinance existing home loans at lower interest rates through a streamlined VA process.
summary: This bill lets eligible veterans refinance their home loans through a simpler VA process. The goal is to lower monthly payments when interest rates fall and make the refinancing path easier to use.

title: Water Infrastructure Modernization Act
bill_text: A BILL To support drinking water upgrades, lead pipe replacement, and wastewater treatment improvements.
summary: This bill provides federal support for water system upgrades, including lead pipe replacement and wastewater improvements. It is meant to improve public health and keep aging infrastructure in working order.

title: Election Security Assistance Act
bill_text: A BILL To fund state and local election security improvements, including paper backups and cyber training.
summary: This bill helps state and local election offices improve security by funding paper backups, cyber training, and related safeguards. The goal is to make election systems more resilient and trustworthy.

title: Public Transit Reliability Act
bill_text: A BILL To improve transit frequency, station maintenance, and accessibility for buses and rail systems.
summary: This bill aims to make public transit more reliable by funding better service frequency, station maintenance, and accessibility upgrades. Riders should see fewer delays and better service quality if the bill is implemented.
